mechanism_narrative: A persistently low unemployment rate signals a late-cycle economy where labor market tightness may lead to wage inflation and margin compression for corporations, ultimately resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:historical correlation of low unemployment with late-cycle equity performance
failure_condition: If UNRATE remains below 5% for 12 consecutive months and S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 60% of those months, this hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds Rate rises above a critical threshold, it indicates a significant monetary tightening cycle aimed at curbing inflation, which increases the cost of capital and reduces corporate profitability, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: If FEDFUNDS exceeds 1.0% for 3 consecutive months and S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered instances, this hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: Strong and accelerating retail sales combined with robust housing starts indicate broad-based consumer and investment demand, signaling a healthy economic expansion that supports corporate earnings growth and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month average growth > 0.5% AND HOUST 3-month average > 1000k units
evidence_refs: fact:consumer spending and housing as leading indicators
failure_condition: If both RSAFS and HOUST triggers are met, but S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of such periods over a 5-year rolling window, this hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: An initial rebound in industrial production following a period of contraction suggests a recovery in manufacturing and supply chains, indicating improving economic conditions that typically precede positive S&P 500 forward returns over a short 5-day horizon as sentiment shifts.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month growth > 0.5% after 2 consecutive months of decline
evidence_refs: fact:industrial production as a coincident indicator
failure_condition: If INDPRO shows the specified rebound, but S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 60% of triggered instances during a recovery regime, this hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating GDP growth, particularly when it surpasses a certain annualized rate, signifies robust economic activity and strong corporate fundamentals, which typically translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon as investors price in future earnings.
evidence_refs: fact:GDP growth and equity market performance
failure_condition: If GDPC1 growth consistently exceeds 3.0% for two consecutive quarters, yet S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 45% of the subsequent periods, this hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: Persistently high consumer price inflation erodes purchasing power and corporate profit margins, often leading to tighter monetary policy and higher discount rates, which collectively exert downward pressure on equity valuations, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth remains above 4% for 6 consecutive months and S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of those months, this hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strong payroll employment growth and a declining unemployment rate signal a robust and improving labor market, which boosts consumer confidence and spending, driving corporate revenues and leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a short 5-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month change > 150k AND UNRATE month-over-month change < -0.1pp
evidence_refs: fact:strong labor market indicators and equity performance
failure_condition: If both PAYEMS and UNRATE triggers are met, but S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of such periods over any 3-year rolling window, this hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: A significant and sustained decline in housing starts below a critical level indicates a weakening in residential investment and broader economic demand, signaling an impending slowdown that negatively impacts corporate earnings and leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:housing starts as a leading economic indicator
failure_condition: If HOUST remains below 800k units for 3 consecutive months, but S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of the subsequent periods, this hypothesis is falsified.
